summary: buffer-utilities executes remote code from jsonkeeper paste host on require
details: On require(), index.js (lines 45-65) decodes a base64-obfuscated URL to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/PT0ON, fetches the JSON body, and passes data.content directly to eval(), executing attacker-controlled JavaScript inside the installer's Node process. The paste host is anonymous and mutable, so the executed payload can change at any time without any package update. The package masquerades as Feross Aboukhadijeh's legitimate `buffer` module — package.json reuses Feross's author/homepage metadata (github.com/feross/buffer) and index.js carries the real buffer source's MIT/author headers, while README, LICENSE, and AUTHORS are empty. Suspicious unused dependencies (axios, request, execp, fs, path) reinforce the masquerade. Any project that installs and requires `buffer-utilities` will execute whatever JavaScript the attacker currently hosts at the paste URL.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
